rows.locks: The M99 verification report passes only when the imported 2.5D report validates, the observed state hash matches stateHashPrimary, and perceptual SSIM/pixel-diff evidence stays secondary corroboration.
Yellow fidelity rows and gap attribution remain visible for unsupported presentation/import gaps; lossy import facts cannot be silently graded clean.
M99 verification reports keep claimedPortedUnits empty and reject fidelity rows that claim ported, auto-ported, or auto-translated units without captured passing oracle evidence.
A stale or divergent observed state hash fails the primary gate, and tampering with the verification reportHash fails validation.
Perceptual render evidence must satisfy fixture SSIM/pixel-diff tolerance, but the report still records it as secondary-only and never as authoritative state truth.
Coverage v85 records the M99 verification report suite, scripted demo summary, open #1/#23 governance anchors, and exact verification command shape from #2202.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v85 is a Rust data-plane regression suite for Era P M99 2.5D import verification and fidelity reporting. It locks source-project/open-text glTF verification, one-way import to Ouroforge-native skeleton evidence, no foreign runtime bridge, no auto-port or ported claim without a passing oracle, honest Yellow/Red fidelity gap attribution for lossy imports, deterministic state-hash primary behavior including stale-hash rejection, perceptual SSIM/pixel-diff render secondary-only corroboration with tolerance checks, and Rust-owned artifact truth. Elixir/Phoenix Studio is not touched and has no trusted write or artifact semantics authority. #1 and #23 remain open.
